description: Implement a cross-platform audio playback system for Kotlin Multiplatform (Desktop JVM + Android) using the expect/actual pattern. The system should be able to play bark WAV files on demand when a BarkEvent fires, with proper lifecycle management.
steps: Create composeApp/src/commonMain/kotlin/rpg/bark/audio/AudioPlayer.kt - an expect class or interface with methods: play(resourcePath: String), stop(), isPlaying(): Boolean, release(). The resourcePath is relative to composeResources/files/ (e.g. 'bark/nib/i_smell_treasure.wav').

Create composeApp/src/commonMain/kotlin/rpg/bark/audio/BarkAudioPlayer.kt - a higher-level class that integrates with BarkAudioRegistry. It takes a BarkEvent + PartyCharacter, looks up the WAV path from BarkAudioRegistry, and plays it via AudioPlayer. Includes volume control and a way to cancel/interrupt a playing bark.

Create composeApp/src/desktopMain/kotlin/rpg/bark/audio/AudioPlayer.desktop.kt - the actual implementation for JVM Desktop using javax.sound.sampled (Clip/AudioSystem). Load WAV from classpath resources (composeResources files are available on classpath at runtime). Implementation should: open AudioInputStream from the resource, create a Clip, play it, handle completion callbacks.

Create composeApp/src/androidMain/kotlin/rpg/bark/audio/AudioPlayer.android.kt - the actual implementation for Android using android.media.SoundPool or MediaPlayer. Load WAV from assets (composeResources files are bundled as Android assets). Use SoundPool for short clips (barks are typically 1-5 seconds).

Wire BarkAudioPlayer into SliceDirector: when fireBark() results in BarkOutcome.Fired, also trigger audio playback for the bark. The audio is fire-and-forget (non-blocking). Make the audio player optional/injectable so tests still work without audio.

Add composeApp/src/commonTest/kotlin/BarkAudioPlayerTest.kt - test that BarkAudioPlayer correctly resolves WAV paths from BarkAudioRegistry for given BarkEvent+PartyCharacter combinations. Use a mock/no-op AudioPlayer implementation for testing.

Add desktopMain/androidMain dependencies if needed: javax.sound.sampled is part of JDK so no extra dep for desktop. For Android, SoundPool is part of the framework. No external audio libraries needed.
acceptance_criteria: AudioPlayer expect/actual compiles for both desktop and android targets
BarkAudioPlayer resolves correct WAV path for any BarkEvent + PartyCharacter combination
Desktop implementation uses javax.sound.sampled.Clip for WAV playback
Android implementation uses SoundPool or MediaPlayer for WAV playback
SliceDirector optionally triggers audio on bark fire without breaking existing tests
BarkAudioPlayerTest passes verifying path resolution logic
verification: Run ./gradlew allTests and verify all tests pass
Run ./gradlew build to verify compilation for both desktop and android targets
findings: Used interface-based AudioPlayer (not expect/actual class) because commonTest needs to create fake implementations directly. SliceDirector.barkAudioPlayer is a public var property rather than constructor param to avoid breaking trailing-lambda call sites (Kotlin compiler issue with SAM-like resolution). Android SDK not available in sandbox so ./gradlew build fails; ./gradlew desktopTest confirms all 11 test classes pass. Android code uses standard framework APIs and will compile with SDK present. Review fixes applied: AudioPlayer.play() now accepts optional onComplete callback; DesktopAudioPlayer uses synchronized block to prevent clip double-close race; AndroidAudioPlayer documents streaming alternative via AssetFileDescriptor.
